title: [Feature Request] Missing a package manager database update
author: lrntgr
body:
Executing `metapac sync` (or `metapac update-all`) can sometimes fail due to the underlying backend database needing an update.  
From then, the user must manually fix the underlying package manager: `metapac` on its own cannot handle this (?).

For instance, with the `apt` backend the following error message is encountered:
```
E: Unable to fetch some archives, maybe run apt-get update or try with --fix-missing?
```
Indeed, executing `apt-get update` and re-running the failing `metapac` command is successful.

---

It would be great if `metapac` could expose a sub-command for this, something like `metapac update-backend`, asbtracting commands like:
- `apt-get update`
- `brew update`
- `dnf makecache`
- `pacman --sync --refresh`
- ... etc.

title: metapac clean marks arch packages as removed even if the backend fails to remove them
author: foonly
body:
If you remove packages from your groups and run `metapac clean` and answer "Yes" to the prompt, but then paru gets interrupted, or you answer "no" to the Paru prompt, metapac marks the packages as removed, even though they are still installed on the system.

I don't know if this is strictly a problem in metapac, or if it's more a pacman / paru thing, but it's quite difficult to recover from.

This can be reproduced by removing a package or group from the metapac configuration, then run `metapac clean`, answer yes to the metapac prompt, and then no the the paru prompt.
Nothing is removed from the system, then run `metapac clean` again and you get the "nothing to clean since there are no unmanaged packages" message.

If you then re-install the packages though metapac, clean works again. But if you reinstall the packages manually with pacman it doesn't work.

title: [Feature Request] Support manual dependencies via `uv tool install ... --with ...` for uv
author: proItheus
body:
We can manually inject packages into the env of a tool by 
```
uv tool install markitdown --with ipython # this will add ipython to the env of markitdown
```

We can get the existing `with` deps wih
```
$ uv tool list --show-with

markitdown v0.1.5 [with: ipython]
- markitdown
```

title: [Feature Request] support package extras for uv backend
author: proItheus
body:
Python packages support `extra`s for optional dependencies. E.g. `uv tool install markitdown[pdf]` will install the `markitdown` with `pdf` extra. 

Currently, use `markitdown[pdf]` as package name in metapac works for installation, but once installed, metapac can't know it, and still try to install it each time. `metapac unmanaged` shows `markitdown` package in uv.

```
$ metapac unmanaged

uv = { packages = ["markitdown"] }
```

```
$ metapac sync

uv = { packages = ["markitdown[pdf]"] }
these repos/packages will be installed, do you want to continue? yes
`markitdown[pdf]` is already installed <-- This line is output by uv itself
```

We can get the list with extras by
```
$ uv tool list --show-extras

markitdown v0.1.5 [extras: pdf]
- markitdown
```

title: Brew 5.1.3 quarantine not supported anymore
author: JulienRaynal
body:
The `--quarantine` and `--no-quarantine` options are not supported anymore by brew:
`Error: Calling the `--[no-]quarantine` switch is disabled! There is no replacement.`

This makes `metapac` crash when installing new packages with `metapac sync`.

The solution is probably to just completely remove it from the code :)

title: `metapac unmanaged` should have an option to exclude transitive dependencies from pacman/arch
author: avagordon01
body:
`metapac unmanaged` should have an option to exclude transitive dependencies from pacman/arch.

e.g.: when generating my toml group configs, I used `metapac unmanaged` and because I have `plasma-meta` installed, it lists every transitively installed package of `plasma-meta`, which is quite long and uninteresting to me

I would like to use `metapac unmanaged --arch-no-transitive` or
```
[arch]
unmanaged_exclude_transitive = true
```
and only see `plasma-meta` listed

title: Feature request: add a `--json` option to backends
author: LeonardoMor
body: I'd be nice if `metapac backends --json` printed the backends info in json format to stdout.

title: [Backend] Nix backend
author: maax3v3
body:
Hi,

I'd like to add a Nix backend to Metapac.

I will be working on it. I will figure out a good way of doing it, then I will make a PR when the solution is implemented.

title: Apt 'repos' for ppas
author: rubankopli
body:
It would be nice to be able to add `apt` ppas using the `repos` key in a group file. 
Currently, it look like the [apt backend does not support the 'repos' key](https://github.com/ripytide/metapac/blob/main/src/backends/apt.rs#L154). I would be open to working on a PR for the implementation, but wanted to open a discussion first to gather any suggestions or input, and to make sure that this actually aligns with the project's goals.
Thanks!

title: Support for a lock file
author: JulienRaynal
body:
Hello,

I was asking myself as I actively use this package, if you think the way it was written it could support a lock file with the version of the package and the url.
The idea would be to be able to, using a lockfile, fully restore an environment if any packages break when updating (kinda like pixi: [documentation](https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/workspace/lockfile/)).

If yes, how difficult would you access it to be ? If it's not a too arduous task I would be open to implement it eventually.

title: `*_install` hooks are called after every `sync`
author: smarrk
body:
Hi,

since v0.9.0 `*_install` hooks seem to behave like `*_sync` hooks and are called after every `sync`, even if the packet is already installed.

The behavior can be reproduced with this minimal setup:

```toml
# /tmp/metapac_demo/config.toml
enabled_backends = ["arch"]

[arch]
package_manager = "yay"
```

```toml
# /tmp/metapac_demo/groups/default.toml
[arch]
packages = [
  {
    name = "metapac",
    hooks = {
      before_install = ["echo", "before_install triggered"],
      after_install = ["echo", "after_install triggered"]
    }
  },
]
```

Which creates this output:

```
~ $ RUST_LOG=trace metapac --config-dir=/tmp/metapac_demo sync
 DEBUG metapac::cmd > running command: "yay" with args: ["--sync", "--list", "--quiet"]
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command took 0.82 seconds
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command succeeded, status: exit status: 0
 DEBUG metapac::cmd > running command: "yay" with args: ["--query", "--quiet"]
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command took 0.02 seconds
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command succeeded, status: exit status: 0
 DEBUG metapac::cmd > running command: "mise" with args: ["search", "--no-headers", "--quiet"]
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command took 0.00 seconds
 DEBUG metapac::cmd > running command: "yay" with args: ["--version"]
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command took 0.02 seconds
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command succeeded, status: exit status: 0
 DEBUG metapac::cmd > running command: "yay" with args: ["--query", "--explicit", "--quiet"]
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command took 0.04 seconds
 TRACE metapac::cmd > command succeeded, status: exit status: 0
 INFO  metapac::core > nothing to install as there are no missing packages
 DEBUG metapac::cmd  > running command: "echo" with args: ["before_install triggered"]
before_install triggered
 TRACE metapac::cmd  > command took 0.00 seconds
 TRACE metapac::cmd  > command succeeded, status: exit status: 0
 DEBUG metapac::cmd  > running command: "echo" with args: ["after_install triggered"]
after_install triggered
 TRACE metapac::cmd  > command took 0.00 seconds
 TRACE metapac::cmd  > command succeeded, status: exit status: 0
```

title: Removal of add, remove, install and uninstall subcommands
author: ripytide
body:
This is an issue to discuss the removal of these four command in `metapac` `v0.9.0`.

I could have chosen to spend the time fixing the code so it works with the new group file format but I instead chose to remove it since in my opinion it does complicate both the code, and the documentation and I find that I never actually use the commands myself, I always end up manually editing my group files and then running `metapac sync` or `metapac clean` or even copying the output of `metapac unmanaged` into a new group file. Also with the new repos in group files it feels unsymmetrical to have commands to work on packages but not repos, but then adding another 4 subcommands for working with repos seems excessive.

But I know I'm not the only user of `metapac` so I've preemptively opened this issue to allow people to give feedback on their usage, and if there is enough users with good use-cases then I will consider working to add them back.
